methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing ostracod shell samples for oxygen isotope analysis to improve chronological resolution.

steps.description: 8g dry sediment was freeze-dried and treated with 3% H2O2.
steps.searchable_summary: freeze-dry sediment and treat with hydrogen peroxide

steps.description: Samples were wet-sieved with a mesh size of 250 µm.
Ostracod shells were picked under a binocular microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: pick ostracod shells using a microscope

methods.description: Procedure for analyzing oxygen isotopes in ostracod shells using mass spectrometry.

steps.description: Four to six adult shells (about 20 µg) were used for oxygen isotope analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: select adult ostracod shells for analysis

steps.description: Shells were reacted with 105% phosphoric acid at 70°C using a Kiel IV online carbonate preparation line connected to a MAT 253 mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: react shells with phosphoric acid and analyze with mass spectrometer

steps.description: Reproducibility was checked by replicate analysis of NBS19 and was better than ±0.06 ‰ (1 σ) for δ18O values.

methods.description: Method for estimating ages of new oxygen isotope data and applying lowpass filters to reduce variabilities.

steps.description: Age estimates for the new oxygen isotope data (vs. PDB) were extracted from the existing Sidi Ali age model.
steps.searchable_summary: extract age estimates from Sidi Ali age model

steps.description: Apply 1000 yr and 500 yr lowpass filters (programme PAST) that reduce centennial-scale variabilities of the proxy records.
steps.searchable_summary: apply lowpass filters to proxy records
